task_completion.description: Verify that a folder named 'Handoff' has been created in the email client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A folder named 'Handoff' (case-insensitive) is visible in the folder pane/sidebar of the email client.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 8 emails have been moved into the 'Handoff' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Handoff' folder is selected and its contents are displayed, showing a list of at least 8 distinct emails.

task_completion.description: Verify that the emails in the 'Handoff' folder come from at least 3 different mailing lists.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Looking at the emails inside the 'Handoff' folder, the subject lines (e.g., tags like [SAdev], [ILUG]) or sender addresses indicate they originate from at least 3 distinct mailing lists.

task_completion.description: Verify that an email is being drafted to the backup colleague.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window is open (or an email is saved in the Drafts folder) with the 'To' field explicitly set to 'backup@techcorp.org'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drafted email has an appropriate subject line indicating a handoff or briefing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The subject line of the drafted email contains at least one of the approved keywords (e.g., 'Handoff', 'Briefing', 'Handover', 'Leave', 'Coverage', 'Transition', 'Out of office').

task_completion.description: Verify that the drafted email body mentions at least 2 specific mailing lists.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body of the drafted email explicitly names at least 2 of the known mailing lists (e.g., 'SAdev', 'ILUG', 'zzzzteana', 'satalk', 'exmh', 'irr', 'spamassassin').

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used a graphical email client to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a genuine GUI email client (like Thunderbird or Evolution) being used to manage folders and draft emails, rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the emails in the Handoff folder are genuine emails from the inbox.
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails in the 'Handoff' folder have realistic timestamps, senders, and subjects that match the context of technical mailing lists, rather than being manually typed mockups.

integrity.description: Verify that the email was drafted using the email client's composition interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard email composition window or draft preview is visible, showing no signs of terminal commands (like 'mail' or 'sendmail') being used to bypass the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The folder must be named 'Handoff' (case-insensitive). The Handoff folder must contain at least 8 emails. These emails must originate from at least 3 distinct mailing lists (identifiable by subject tags or sender addresses). The drafted email must be addressed to 'backup@techcorp.org'. The drafted email's subject must contain at least one of the following keywords: 'handoff', 'handover', 'leave', 'coverage', 'transition', 'briefing', 'out of office', or 'out-of-office'. The drafted email body must mention at least 2 of the known mailing lists (e.g., 'sadev', 'ilug', 'zzzzteana', 'satalk', 'exmh', 'irr', 'spamassassin').
